creator: tobik@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Dimitry Andric from comment #11)
Here is a simple reproducer.

$ cat bt.c
#include <execinfo.h>
int main() {
        void *addrlist[100];
        backtrace(addrlist, 100);
}

Compile it on FreeBSD 12.2 (ref12-aarch64.freebsd.org):
$ cc -o bt bt.c -lexecinfo
$ ./bt
$

Copy to install of FreeBSD 14 with LLVM12 and run it
(using 20210701-c5f4772c66d-247671 aarch64 snapshot running in QEMU)
$ ./bt 
Segmentation Fault (core dumped)

Does not crash when LD_PRELOADing libgcc_s.so.1 from 12.2:
$ env LD_PRELOAD=libgcc_s.so.1.12.2 ./bt
$
